description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray coloration interspersed with lighter and darker speckled patterns, suggesting a granite or gneiss composition. The surface is rough and uneven, with jagged edges and a natural, unpolished appearance. One side of the rock features a small, rectangular label or tag, indicating it may have been cataloged or studied. The rock's mass and density are apparent from its solid, blocky form, and it appears heavy and difficult to move without assistance. Its size and shape make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area. The rock does not have any visible cavities or openings and is not shaped to serve as a receptacle.
description: A rough, gray speckled rock with jagged edges and a solid, heavy appearance, featuring a small label on one side. Its natural, unpolished surface makes it suitable as a barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: The rock is seen from its broadest face, showing a mottled gray surface and a small white label attached near one edge.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the rock's triangular profile and the visible thickness of its body, with the label still partially visible.
description_view_2: The rock appears more elongated and tapers towards one end, with the surface texture and speckling clearly visible.
description_view_3: A side view showing the rock's narrowest profile, emphasizing its length and the rough, uneven surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
